outcome_text: Targeted application scope is specified and documented based on the system's capability, established context, and AI system requirements.
scoring_text: Skill in defining and documenting AI system application scope, including specifying deployment boundaries based on system capabilities, aligning scope with established operational context, and documenting scope constraints derived from AI system requirements.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in defining and documenting AI system application scope, including specifying deployment boundaries based on system capabilities, aligning scope with established operational context, and documenting scope constraints derived from AI system requirements.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI governance framework components, including AI system registries, tiered review processes, impact assessment methodologies, and post-deployment monitoring requirements.
comments: NIST AI RMF MP-3.3 (MAP) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-006 is the strongest match (STS: 0.559, cosine: 0.665). Secondary: RM-T-004 (RM, cosine: 0.630), AG-S-008 (AG, cosine: 0.626).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.6/10 (raw 0.559). Shared vocabulary: [ai, deployment, requirements, system]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [aligning, application, based, boundaries, capabilities, constraints] vs WIDAI terms [assessment, components, framework, governance, impact, knowledge] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.885 (P=0.886, R=0.884). Balanced P/R — the 32-word competency implication and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.2%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.665 > STS 0.559 (gap +0.107). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, deployment, requirements, system] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.4%. Shared tokens: [ai, deployment, requirements, system]. NIST AI RMF-only: [aligning, application, based, boundaries, capabilities, constraints]. WIDAI-only: [assessment, components, framework, governance, impact, knowledge].
